$comment: The done-contract IS the product spec. It is authored and adversarially reviewed (challenged for vagueness, unfalsifiable checks, and missing acceptance items) BEFORE the build/verify loop runs. Each item's check must be objective and machine-checkable so the loop can decide pass/fail without re-litigating intent. A Workflow script consumes these field names verbatim, so do not rename them.
title: Goalkeeper Done-Contract

goal.description: One sentence stating what "finished" means for this task. This is the headline the acceptance items collectively prove.

items.description: Acceptance items. Each is independently checkable and contributes one objective pass/fail signal to the loop.

id.description: Short kebab-case identifier, unique within the contract (e.g. "middleware-exists").

priority.description: Optional execution priority. LOWER = higher priority; 1 is done first. Within the set of items whose dependencies are all satisfied, the loop runs the lowest-priority number first. Defaults to a large value when omitted, so explicitly-prioritized items go first.

description.description: What to build or verify for this item, in plain language.

expectedOutput.description: Optional. The concrete artifact or observable result that proves this item is done (e.g. "a 429 response with a Retry-After header on the (N+1)th request"). Distinct from `check`: the check is how the loop measures pass/fail; expectedOutput is what the builder is aiming to produce.

dependsOn.description: Optional. Item ids that MUST pass before this item is eligible to run. Enables dependency-ordered scheduling: the loop only picks an item once every id listed here is already passing. Use [] or omit for an independent item. A dependsOn id that is not present in the contract triggers a dependency-deadlock escalation.

check.description: The objective, machine-checkable acceptance test. Exactly ONE payload field is expected, selected by `type`: type=command -> `command`; type=file_exists -> `path`; type=grep -> `pattern` (matched against the file/output at `path`); type=judge -> `rubric`.

type.description: Which kind of check this is. Determines which payload field is required.

command.description: For type=command: a shell command. Exit code 0 = pass, non-zero = fail.

path.description: For type=file_exists: a path that must exist. Also used by type=grep as the file to match `pattern` against.

pattern.description: For type=grep: a regular expression that must match (typically within the file at `path`).

rubric.description: For type=judge: criteria an LLM judge applies to score the item pass or fail. Reserve for checks that genuinely cannot be expressed as a deterministic command.

allowTestEdit.description: Whether the builder may modify the files this check depends on. Default false. When false, the builder must satisfy the check as written and may NOT edit the check's files to make it pass (no moving the goalposts).
